div.p: Key-Value caching for efficient autoregressive generation

Key Insight: Keys and values for earlier positions don't change when generating new tokens. Cache stores them to avoid recomputation. Each generation step computes K,V only for the new token, then concatenates with cached K,V before attention. Complexity drops from O(n³) to O(n²).

g.text: # Key: cached_K + new_K [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
# Value: cached_V + new_V [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
K = cat([cached_K, new_K]) # [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
V = cat([cached_V, new_V]) # [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
scores = Q @ K.T / sqrt(d_k) # [B, heads, 1, t+1]

text.tspan: # Key: cached_K + new_K [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
# Value: cached_V + new_V [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
K = cat([cached_K, new_K]) # [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
V = cat([cached_V, new_V]) # [B, heads, t+1, d_k]
scores = Q @ K.T / sqrt(d_k) # [B, heads, 1, t+1]
